MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-02-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO ORDER THE REQUESTED PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. IN PENA V. TONEY (1979) 98 CAL.APP.3D 534, 539, THE CASE CITED BY PLAINTIFF AS PRECEDENT FOR GRANTING SUCH RELIEF, THE COURT DECLINED TO COMMENT ON THE PROPRIETY OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. EVEN IF THE COURT HAD AUTHORITY, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE BASED ON THE EVIDENCE PROVIDED AND WITH THE AMOUNT OF CERTAINTY REQUIRED FOR THE DRASTIC REMEDY OF PREVENTING DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEYS FROM BEING PAID UNTIL THIS CASE IS REDUCED TO JUDGMENT, THAT DEFENDANT IS PAYING HER ATTORNEYS WITH EMBEZZLED FUNDS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT MONICA TAYLOR AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED; NO OPPOSITION. DEFENDANT TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON OR BEFORE NOVEMBER 30, 2014 AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS. NOTHING IN THIS ORDER SHALL PREVENT DEFENDANT FROM ASSERTING ANY PRIVILEGES AT THE TIME OF THE DEPOSITION. SANCTIONS AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1060 AGAINST DEFENDANT. FAILURE TO APPEAR WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE MAY RESULT IN STRIKING OF ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM ROBERT F. KANE, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED (302).

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT TAYLOR, MONICA SERGENT, JAMES L. AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA JL SERGENT CONSULTING $621,577.00 PRINCIPAL, $14,140.92 INTEREST, $3,501.42 COSTS, $2,000,000.00 PUNITIVE DAMAGES, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,639,219.34, OTHER: PLAINTIFF IS ALSO ENTITLED TO GRANT OF PERMANENT INJUNCTION AGAINST TAYLOR AND "SERGENT" PERMANENTLY ENJOINING THEM FROM ACCESSING FUNDS IN THE COMMUNITY TRUST ACCOUNTS UNDER HER AND SERGENT'S NAME AND THE CITIBANK ACCOUNTS AND WELLS FARGO ACCOUNTS UNDER HER NAME (AS IDENTIFIED ON ATTACHED EXHIBIT "A") AND TO HAVE FUNDS IN THOSE ACCOUNTS RELEASED TO PROLOGIS WITHIN 10 COURT DAY OF ENTRY OF THIS JUDGMENT. calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Prologis, Inc.S Notice Of And Request For Prove-Up Hearing For Entry Of Court Judgment, Or Alternatively, Entry Of Court Judgment On The Papers Against Monica Taylor
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

John T. Cu, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, PROLOGIS, INC.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) MONICA TAYLOR and JAMES L. SERGENT, an individual and dba JL SERGENT CONSULTING ("SERGENT")
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Damages: $621,577.00
Pre-Judgment Interest AT THE ANNUAL RATE OF 7%: $14,140.92
Attorney's Fees: $0.00
Costs: $3,501.42
Other: Punitive Damages: $2,000,000.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $2,639,219.34
Other: Plaintiff is also entitled to grant of permanent injunction against Taylor and "Sergent" permanently enjoining them from accessing funds in the Community Trust Accounts under her and Sergent's name and the Citibank Accounts and Wells Fargo Accounts under her name (as identified on attached Exhibit "A") and to have funds in those accounts released to ProLogis within 10 court day of entry of this Judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION / Ntc Of Mo & Mo To Compel Deposition Of Deft Monica Taylor And For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Russell C. Petersen, Esq., 415.777.3200, for plaintiff Prologis Inc.; No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INC.'s MOTION To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Monica Taylor And For Sanctions is granted; no opposition. Defendant to appear for deposition on or before November 30, 2014 and produce documents. Nothing in this order shall prevent Defendant from asserting any privileges at the time of the deposition. Sanctions awarded in the amount of $1060 against defendant. Failure to appear without good cause may result in striking of answer. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem Robert F. Kane, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported (302).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INCORPORATED'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is Denied. Plaintiff has not established that the Court has the authority to order the requested preliminary injunction. In Pena v. Toney (1979) 98 Cal.App.3d 534, 539, the case cited by Plaintiff as precedent for granting such relief, the court declined to comment on the propriety of the preliminary injunction. Even if the Court had authority, the Court cannot determine based on the evidence provided and with the amount of certainty required for the drastic remedy of preventing Defendant's attorneys from being paid until this case is reduced to judgment, that Defendant is paying her attorneys with embezzled funds. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976
